submissions.body:
Hi,

This is the code from the actually completed assignment: 

 

/*
🎯 PARAMETERS & ARGUMENTS PRACTICE EXERCISES 🎯

Welcome to your auto-graded JavaScript exercise!

INSTRUCTIONS:
- Complete each function according to the specifications
- Run the tests to see if your solutions work (Tests tab on the right)
- Green checkmarks = correct, red X's = needs work
- Get specific feedback for each test that fails
- Don't move on until all tests pass!

CODESANDBOX TIPS:
- Your code goes in this file (index.js)
- Tests automatically run when you save
- Check the "Tests" tab to see your progress
- Console output appears in the "Console" tab

Let's start coding! 🚀
*/

// =================================================================
// EXERCISE TYPE 1: STEP-BY-STEP BUILDING
// =================================================================

/**
* Exercise 1A: Pet Feeder
* Create a function that takes a pet name and food amount as parameters
* Should return a string: "Feeding [petName] [amount] pieces of food. [petName] is happy!"
*/
export function feedPet(petName, amount) {
// TODO: Replace this with your implementation
// Example: feedPet("Fluffy", 3) should return "Feeding Fluffy 3 pieces of food. Fluffy is happy!"
let answer = `Feeding ${petName} ${amount} pieces of food. ${petName} is happy!`;
return answer;
}

/**
* Exercise 1B: Grade Calculator
* Create a function that takes student name, score, and class name
* Should return: "[studentName] scored [score] points in [className]"
*/
export function announceGrade(studentName, score, className) {
// TODO: Implement this function
// Example: announceGrade("Sarah", 85, "Math") should return "Sarah scored 85 points in Math"
return `${studentName} scored ${score} points in ${className}`;
}

/**
* Exercise 1C: Weather Report
* Create a function that takes day, city, temperature, and condition
* Should return: "[day] in [city]: [temp] degrees and [condition]. Have a great day!"
*/
export function weatherReport(day, city, temperature, condition) {
// TODO: Implement this function
// Example: weatherReport("Monday", "Oakland", 72, "sunny")
// should return "Monday in Oakland: 72 degrees and sunny. Have a great day!"
return `${day} in ${city}: ${temperature} degrees and ${condition}. Have a great day!`;
}

// =================================================================
// EXERCISE TYPE 2: CONVERT STATIC TO DYNAMIC
// =================================================================

/**
* Exercise 2A: Social Media Post
* Convert the hardcoded function below to use parameters
* Should return a formatted post string
*/
export function createPost(user, message, platform) {
// TODO: Make this function use the parameters instead of hardcoded values
// Currently hardcoded:
//let hardcodedUser = "jenny_codes";
//let hardcodedMessage = "Just learned JavaScript!";
// let hardcodedPlatform = "Twitter";

// Should return: "New post on [platform]\n@[user]: [message]\n❤️ Like | 🔄 Share"
return (
"New post on " +
platform +
"\n@" +
user +
": " +
message +
"\n❤️ Like | 🔄 Share"
);
}

/**
* Exercise 2B: Shopping Cart
* Convert this to use parameters for item, price, and quantity
* Should return: "Added to cart: [item]\nPrice: $[price] x [quantity]\nSubtotal: $[total]"
*/
export function addToCart(item, price, quantity) {
// TODO: Replace hardcoded values with parameters
//let hardcodedItem = "laptop";
// let hardcodedPrice = 899;
// let hardcodedQuantity = 1;
let total = price * quantity;

return (
"Added to cart: " +
item +
"\nPrice: $" +
price +
" x " +
quantity +
"\nSubtotal: $" +
total
);
}

/**
* Exercise 2C: Movie Recommendation
* Make this work for any user, movie, genre, and rating
* Should return formatted recommendation string
*/
export function recommendMovie(userName, movieTitle, genre, rating) {
// TODO: Use parameters instead of hardcoded values
//let hardcodedUser = "Alex";
// let hardcodedMovie = "The Matrix";
// let hardcodedGenre = "Sci-Fi";
// let hardcodedRating = 9.2;

return (
"Hey " +
userName +
"!\nYou might like: " +
movieTitle +
"\nGenre: " +
genre +
" | Rating: " +
rating +
"/10\nHappy watching! 🍿"
);
}

// =================================================================
// EXERCISE TYPE 3: FUNCTION MATCHING PUZZLE
// =================================================================

/**
* Exercise 3A: Calculate Tip
* Write a function that calculates tip amount
* Should return: "Tip for $[bill] at [percentage]%: $[tipAmount]"
*/
export function calculateTip(billAmount, tipPercentage) {
// TODO: Calculate tip and return formatted string
//to calculate tip: billAmount * tipPercentage / 100
let tipAmount = (billAmount * tipPercentage) / 100;
// let answer = `Tip for $${bill} at ${percentage}%: $${tipAmount}`;
// Example: calculateTip(50, 18) should return "Tip for $50 at 18%: $9"
//return answer;

return "Tip for $" + billAmount + " at " + tipPercentage + "%: $" + tipAmount;
}

/**
* Exercise 3B: Send Email
* Write a function that formats an email
* Should return: "To: [recipient]\nSubject: [subject]\nMessage: [body]"
*/
export function sendEmail(recipient, subject, body) {
// TODO: Format and return email string
return "To: " + recipient + "\nSubject: " + subject + "\nMessage: " + body;
}

/**
* Exercise 3C: Roll Dice
* Write a function that simulates rolling a die
* Should return: "Rolling a [sides]-sided die: [result]" where result is random 1 to sides
*/
export function rollDice(sides) {
// TODO: Generate random number from 1 to sides and return formatted string
// Hint: Math.floor(Math.random() * sides) + 1
let result = Math.floor(Math.random() * sides) + 1;
return `Rolling a ${sides}-sided die: ${result}`;
}

// =================================================================
// EXERCISE TYPE 4: DEBUG THE BUGS
// =================================================================

/**
* Exercise 4A: Greet User (Missing Parameters)
* FIX: This function is called with 2 arguments but only accepts 1 parameter
* Should return: "Hello [name]! Welcome to [website]!"
*/
export function greetUser(name, website) {
// TODO: Add missing parameter and fix the function
// This should work: greetUser("Sarah", "CodeAcademy")
return "Hello " + name + "! Welcome to " + website + "!";
}

/**
* Exercise 4B: Book Flight (Missing Parameters)
* FIX: This function needs more parameters to match how it's being called
* Should return: "Booking flight to [destination] on [date] for [passengers] passengers. Cost: $[cost]"
*/
export function bookFlight(
destination,
departureDate,
passengerCount,
totalCost
) {
// TODO: Add missing parameters
// This should work: bookFlight("Paris", "2024-06-15", 2, 800)
return (
"Booking flight to " +
destination +
" on " +
departureDate +
" for " +
passengerCount +
" passengers. Cost: $" +
totalCost
);
}

/**

/**
* Exercise 4D: Order Pizza (Remove Unused Parameters)
* FIX: This function has too many parameters for how it's being used
* Should only need: size, toppings, crust
* Should return: "Ordering [size] pizza with [toppings] on [crust] crust"
*/
export function orderPizza(size, toppings, crust) {
// TODO: Remove unused parameters and fix the function
// Should work: orderPizza("large", "pepperoni", "thin")
return (
"Ordering " + size + " pizza with " + toppings + " on " + crust + " crust"
);
}

// =================================================================
// BONUS CHALLENGES (Optional)
// =================================================================

/**
* BONUS 1: Create Playlist
* Write a function that creates a music playlist
* Should return: "Created playlist '[name]' with [songCount] [genre] songs"
*/
export function createPlaylist(playlistName, songCount, genre) {
// TODO: Implement bonus function
return `Created playlist '${playlistName}' with ${songCount} ${genre} songs`;
}

/**
* BONUS 2: Format Phone Number
* Write a function that formats a phone number with dashes
* Should return: "XXX-XXX-XXXX" format
*/
export function formatPhoneNumber(phoneNumber) {
// TODO: Format phone number string
// Example: formatPhoneNumber("5551234567") should return "555-123-4567"
return (
phoneNumber.slice(0, 3) +
"-" +
phoneNumber.slice(3, 6) +
"-" +
phoneNumber.slice(6)
);
}

/*
🎯 TESTING YOUR CODE:
1. Save this file to run the tests automatically
2. Check the "Tests" tab to see your progress
3. Green checkmarks mean your functions work correctly
4. Red X's show what needs to be fixed
5. Click on failed tests to see specific error messages
Good luck! 🚀
*/

submission_comments.comment:
Hey Jana, great work with your Coded Dog API App! I see there are some new methods we haven't learned in class which is amazing! We love to see students learning new methods outside of class.
To help us better understand your approach, I’d love to see some comments explaining why you chose certain methods like .value and .trim. You're doing a great job!✨📝

Hi Xavier!  Thank you for the feedback 😊  I used .value to access the actual value that the user enters into the input field or selects from the dropdown. When we select an element with something like document.getElementById(), JavaScript only gives us the HTML element itself. To get the text inside an input field or the selected option in a  element, we need to use .value. That way I can store the user’s search input and selected temperament in variables and use them later when filtering the dog data from the API.
I also used .trim() to remove any extra spaces from the beginning or end of the user’s input. This helps avoid search issues if someone accidentally types a space before or after the breed name. I combined this with 
.toLowerCase() so the search is case-insensitive and works more reliably when comparing the user input with the dog names in the API data. I did a bit of extra research on how form inputs are usually handled in real-world applications so I could understand which methods are commonly used and useful when working with forms. I’ll add more comments in the code to make these choices clearer as well and push it back up to GitHub

Hey Arianne!Sending this back to you because when I try to fill out the form for any dog, nothing comes back/I don't see any results. I tried "pug", terrier" and "hound". Does the application work for you?

Hey Arianne, this looks great so far! The Dog API App seems to only work when both the breed and the temperament work. How do we make it so the user can see either the temperament or the dog breed.** Hint hint:
- How do we structure our functions- What methods do we use to predict what a user might input based on our code?- If our Dog API App only shows when a user inputs both the temperament and the dog breed...how do we structure our statements inside our functions to react situationally when a user only writes one input?-Try testing out these three cases:    -Both temperament and breed     -Only temperament     -Only breed**Hope this helps- if you have any questions, feel free to let us know in the fe-help-channel. You're doing a great job Arianne!

submission_comments.comment: Excellent work Ysabel! Everything looks great and it's cool to see you tinkered with encodeURIComponent in your APU url. Please add a comment in your code explaining its use and how it work

Your comment perfectly explains the URL construction - great job! However, it does not explain the encodeURIComponent part. When we return an assignment asking to include a comment, it is meant for students to add comments for concepts they learnt outside of class. This helps instructors ensure students understand the code they have written and helps the student remember what it means and how it works the next time you look at it.
